title: groundstation field test №2 — the wave edition, actually run
heading: THE FIELD TEST №2
sub: groundstation · 10 prompts · one day of new features · July 22, 2026 · Development Seed labs
lede: Every example below ran for real against live data on July 22: real STAC searches, real event feeds, real weather, real artifacts you can open. The focus is everything that shipped in the last day — 3D fly-throughs, auto-mosaics, postcards, the stack layer, local-model synthesis, scheduled sweeps — pointed at what is actually happening on Earth this week.

stats.label: live wildfires on one Oregon map
API keys needed for any of it

rounds.name: Round 1 — the new artifacts, pointed at this week's Earth

cases.prompt: Show me the terrain around the BROOK fire in Shasta County in 3D — I want to understand what the crews are working in.
cases.answer: Found the live EONET fire, picked a 0.01%-cloud July 7 scene at 98.2% coverage, and draped it over real terrain at 1.8× exaggeration. The canyon country east of the Sacramento Valley reads instantly — this is steep ground.
cases.shows: render_map_3d, new today: live event → terrain answer in one call

cases.prompt: Give me a 3D fly-through of Torres del Paine.
cases.answer: Southern-hemisphere winter light on the towers: 1.6% cloud, 96.5% coverage, July 10. Exaggeration slider, orbiting fly-through, reset view — all in a self-contained page with keyless terrain tiles that shares as-is.
cases.shows: the page that used to be hand-written per request is now one tool call

cases.prompt: Show me Calgary. All of it. And show me what's behind the map.
cases.answer: Calgary spans two UTM zones, so no single scene covers it. The search returned a same-day two-tile set at 100% union coverage without being asked twice, and the Stack toggle reveals the pipeline: Earth Search → COG range requests → TiTiler serving sentinel-2-l2a via titiler.xyz → MapLibre, each with a role badge and a kind-colored dot.
cases.shows: full_coverage_set and stack_layer, both new today, on one artifact

cases.prompt: What's burning in north-central Oregon right now? Map every active fire over current imagery.
cases.answer: Eleven live EONET fires — the Hay Creek Complex, Rowe Creek Complex, and nine more across Gilliam, Sherman, Wasco, and Wheeler counties — as clickable markers over a four-tile same-day mosaic (July 17, 100% union coverage). Weather context: 0.4mm of rain in the past week, 34.5°C ahead.
cases.learning: the first pass used the single best scene — 63.8% coverage. The auto-mosaic turned that into a four-tile 100% answer, which is exactly the reprompt it was built to remove.
cases.shows: auto-mosaic scaling past two tiles on a real fire complex

cases.prompt: Make me a postcard of the country the BROOK fire is burning in.
cases.answer: One card: the July 7 scene as embedded pixels (nothing expires, nothing links out), place, date, caption, and the attribution block — Development Seed labs · STAC · TiTiler · sentinel-2-l2a via Element 84 Earth Search. Posting it anywhere stays a human call.
cases.learning: an iceberg postcard (A76C, South Atlantic) came back empty — Sentinel-2 doesn't acquire open ocean. The honest zero beat a wrong artifact.
cases.shows: render_postcard, new today: durable, attributed, safe to share

rounds.name: Round 2 — analysis, synthesis, and the machinery that runs alone

cases.prompt: NDVI postcard of the Salinas Valley at the height of the growing season.
cases.answer: The valley floor glows green against the dry July hills: NDVI mean 0.331, July 17, 3.3% cloud, rendered with the rdylgn colormap and captioned so the colors explain themselves. Index cards take the same expression machinery as index map layers.
cases.learning: geocoding 'Salinas Valley' returned Salinas Valley State Prison — a 1km box. The region-scale sanity check in the skill caught it, and the AOI was widened to the actual valley.
cases.shows: band math + colormap on a shareable card

cases.prompt: Where is Tropical Storm Bertha, and what does the week look like for the Florida Panhandle coast?
cases.answer: Bertha's EONET position (July 21) mapped against the newest coastal scene, with the forward signal read honestly: winds already easing (51 → 18 km/h) and under 7mm of rain across the whole week ahead — this system is not the panhandle's problem this week. The best coastal scene covers 49.8% and the answer says so.
cases.shows: events + forward weather + coverage honesty in one read

cases.prompt: Brief me on Chelan County — but synthesize it on the local model, not the cloud.
cases.answer: Ollama's qwen2.5-coder:7b wrote a coherent brief on this laptop: the two named wildfires, the NDVI slip, the heat. Then the eval gate failed it — dates and the NDVI number weren't cited to spec — which is the design working: local output gets the same quality bar as cloud output, and a sweep would have withheld it from Slack rather than posting it.
cases.learning: the gate is what makes 'run it locally when possible' safe rather than aspirational. A failing local brief degrades to the claude path, then to the deterministic brief — the chain never returns nothing.
cases.shows: local synthesis + preflight + eval gate, all new today

cases.prompt: Run the morning fleet sweep, dry-run the Slack post.
cases.answer: Four areas triaged in one run: Chelan WATCH (same two uncontained fires, NDVI 0.626 → 0.609 under record heat), Laredo WATCH (day seven of a 42.4°C heat wave, NDVI down 19.6%), Efate CALM. Chelan's brief opened with 'steady state since the last run today' — it remembers yesterday. The payload printed instead of posting.
cases.learning: Barotse's summary read calm but carried a WATCH tag — the alert extraction takes the first CALM/WATCH/ACT match anywhere in the text. Now filed as the next fix, which is the field test doing its job.
cases.shows: lockfile, log, eval-gated posting, day-over-day memory

cases.prompt: bash scripts/doctor.sh — on a machine that just cloned this.
cases.answer: The preflight walks the chain in the order it breaks — uv, server env, CLI, plugin wiring, endpoints, and new today: whether the copy Claude runs is current, printing the exact update command when it's behind. On a TTY it wears the new masthead and status colors; piped, it stays byte-clean plain text.
cases.learning: plugin installs are cached per version, so six merged PRs deliver nothing until the version bumps and the user updates — the doctor now says which copy is running and how to move.
cases.shows: the whole first-run and upgrade path, hardened today

repro:
git clone https://github.com/dannybauman/groundstation && cd groundstation
bash scripts/doctor.sh                    # walks the setup chain, prints the fix
uv run evals/unit_checks.py               # 59 offline checks
uv run evals/run_evals.py                 # live checks against real endpoints
uv run scripts/field_test.py docs/field-test-2026-07-22.cases.json   # rebuild this page
footer: field test №2 · July 22, 2026 · built with the field-test factory (scripts/field_test.py) · groundstation · Development Seed labs
